question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The GDP gap between California and Oklahoma might exceed that between South Dakota and Mississippi.
options.B: California and Oklahoma have similar GDP levels.
options.C: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.D: All four states (California, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Mississippi) have almost identical GDP levels.

explanation: California (420.7$B) and Oklahoma (614.1$B) have a 193.4$B GDP difference, while South Dakota (99.8$B) and Mississippi (100.4$B) have only a 0.6$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups California and Oklahoma into the same color bin (400$B+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while South Dakota and Mississippi appear more different due to different color bins.
